description: A plain screenshot of body text from a news article (Wall Street Journal, per the tweet), set in a serif typeface on a white background with one hyperlinked phrase ('steel and aluminum') shown in blue underlined text. The captured paragraphs report that Ellwood Group, a Pennsylvania maker of forged steel, nickel and aluminum products, saw sales jump 35% from Q4 to Q1 driven in part by tariffs, that customers are 'reshoring' to derisk foreign supply chains, that the Trump administration imposed 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum in March, and that Ellwood is adding staff and anticipates higher profit-sharing. There is no agency design, seal, slogan or graphic overlay—just cropped article text—so this is not a designed meme/poster. The Rapid Response 47 account is using the screenshot as evidence to amplify a pro-tariff economic message. Marked is_designed=false (document/article screenshot).
notable_text: Ellwood Group ... saw sales jump 35% ... driven in part by tariffs ... reshoring because they want to derisk foreign supply chains.
